title: notholaena ferruginea
intro: Notholaena ferruginea, also known as the rusty fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae, characterized by its reddish-brown stems and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Notholaena ferruginea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Notholaena ferruginea may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
